Glucosamine has been extensively studied in osteoarthritis, where it demonstrates modest but clinically meaningful benefits in reducing pain and improving mobility. While there are some theoretical links to other conditions like Alzheimer's due to shared metabolic pathways involving glycosaminoglycans, these remain unproven and unsupported by strong clinical data. The association with cancers such as Niemann-Pick disease is biologically implausible given no known mechanism of action linking glucosamine directly to those diseases.
